findings.issue: buildChildStats(tasks) and flattenVisible() are called on every render without memoization. buildChildStats iterates all tasks to build a Map, then flattenVisible recursively flattens the tree. Neither is wrapped in useMemo, so they recompute on every state change (e.g., expandedIds toggle, drag start/end, any parent re-render).
findings.recommendation: Wrap buildChildStats in useMemo with [tasks] dependency. Wrap flattenVisible result in useMemo with [topLevel, childStatsMap, expandedIds, onAddSubtask] dependencies.

findings.issue: Round 1 claimed to fix the taskCount-driven cascading API calls, but the code still has taskCount in the useEffect dependency array (line 126-130). Every task addition/deletion/completion still triggers fetchProjects + fetchTags + fetchBlockedTaskIds — 3 API calls per task mutation.
findings.recommendation: Remove taskCount from the dependency array. Fetch only on mount and on dataMutationCount changes. The round 1 fix was either not applied or was reverted.

findings.issue: Round 1 claimed to consolidate 4 separate task array scans into a single pass, but the code still has 4 separate useMemo blocks (visibleTasks line 189, inboxTaskCount line 210, todayTaskCount line 214, projectTaskCounts line 218, projectCompletedCounts line 226) — each independently iterating state.tasks. The round 1 fix was either not applied or was reverted.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate into a single useMemo that iterates state.tasks once and returns all derived values: visibleTasks, inboxCount, todayCount, projectCounts, projectCompletedCounts.

findings.issue: defaultDueDate={new Date(today + 'T00:00:00')} creates a new Date object on every render, causing TaskInput to receive a new prop reference each time. Since TaskInput likely uses this for comparison/effects, this triggers unnecessary re-renders of the entire task input subtree on every Today view render.
findings.recommendation: Wrap in useMemo: const defaultDueDate = useMemo(() => new Date(today + 'T00:00:00'), [today]);
findings.code_snippet: defaultDueDate={new Date(today + "T00:00:00")}

findings.issue: EatTheFrog receives [...overdueTasks, ...todayTasks] — a new array created via spread on every render. This forces EatTheFrog (and its selectFrogTask useMemo) to recompute even when neither overdueTasks nor todayTasks have changed.
findings.recommendation: Memoize: const frogCandidates = useMemo(() => [...overdueTasks, ...todayTasks], [overdueTasks, todayTasks]); and pass frogCandidates as the tasks prop.
findings.code_snippet: <EatTheFrog tasks={[...overdueTasks, ...todayTasks]} ... />

findings.issue: Same issue for WorkloadCapacityBar: [...overdueTasks, ...todayTasks] spread creates a new array on every render inside the useMemo dependency chain. The plannedMinutes useMemo depends on overdueTasks and todayTasks directly, but this spread is still wasteful in the EatTheFrog case.
findings.recommendation: Share a single memoized combined array for both EatTheFrog and plannedMinutes calculations.
findings.code_snippet: () => [...overdueTasks, ...todayTasks].reduce((sum, t) => sum + (t.estimatedMinutes ?? 0), 0)

findings.issue: new Date(task.dueDate) < new Date() is evaluated on every render for every task item. This creates two Date objects per task per render. With 100 visible tasks, that's 200 Date allocations per render cycle.
findings.recommendation: Compare dueDate string against a precomputed ISO date string (toDateKey) instead of constructing Date objects. Or memoize the isOverdue check.
findings.code_snippet: const isOverdue = task.dueDate && task.status === "pending" && new Date(task.dueDate) < new Date();

findings.issue: TaskItem has two separate useEffect hooks watching task.status (lines 62 and 102) that both trigger on completion — one for the CSS animation class, one for the CompletionBurst. They duplicate the same status transition detection logic.
findings.recommendation: Combine into a single useEffect that handles both the animClass and showBurst state in one status transition check.

findings.issue: getLast7Days() is called on every render without memoization. It creates 7 Date objects and string arrays each time the component renders, even though the result only changes once per day.
findings.recommendation: Wrap in useMemo with [today] dependency: const last7Days = useMemo(() => getLast7Days(), [today]);

findings.issue: Round 1 reported fixing maxCount with useMemo, but the code still shows it computed inline without memoization: Math.max(...dailyCounts.map(d => d.count), 1). The round 1 fix was either not applied or was reverted.
findings.recommendation: Wrap in useMemo: const maxCount = useMemo(() => Math.max(...dailyCounts.map(d => d.count), 1), [dailyCounts]);
findings.code_snippet: const maxCount = Math.max(...dailyCounts.map((d) => d.count), 1);

findings.issue: WelcomeScreen filters state.tasks 3 times on every render (overdueCount, todayCount, pendingCount) without useMemo. Since WelcomeScreen consumes TaskContext via useTaskContext(), it re-renders on every task state change, recomputing all 3 filters each time.
findings.recommendation: Wrap each count in useMemo with [tasks, todayStr] dependencies, or compute all three in a single useMemo pass.

findings.issue: extractTasksFromMessage() is called during render for every message bubble without memoization. It creates new Set, new Map, and parses JSON.parse for every tool call/result on each render. For conversations with many tool-using messages, this adds up.
findings.recommendation: Memoize the extracted tasks: const inlineTasks = useMemo(() => extractTasksFromMessage(message), [message]);

findings.issue: Inline IIFE in JSX: {(() => { const c = inboxTasks.filter(t => t.status === 'pending').length; return `${c} ...`; })()} runs a filter on every render to count pending tasks. This is a redundant iteration since inboxTasks is already filtered/sorted.
findings.recommendation: Compute pendingCount in a useMemo or derive it from inboxTasks which is already memoized: const pendingCount = useMemo(() => inboxTasks.filter(t => t.status === 'pending').length, [inboxTasks]);

findings.issue: Settings are loaded by issuing N parallel API calls (one per setting key, ~52 keys). This means 52 HTTP requests fire on mount. While parallel, this is still a lot of network overhead.
findings.recommendation: Add a batch settings endpoint (GET /api/settings/all) that returns all settings in one request, or use localStorage as a read-through cache to avoid the waterfall on subsequent loads.
findings.code_snippet: Promise.all(SETTING_KEYS.map((key) => api.getAppSetting(key)))

findings.issue: handleToggle calls tasks.find(t => t.id === id) — O(n) linear scan on the full task list. While tasks is already filtered to quickWins, the find is against the original tasks prop, not quickWins.
findings.recommendation: Search quickWins instead of tasks, or build a Set of quickWin IDs for O(1) lookup.
findings.code_snippet: const task = tasks.find((t) => t.id === id);

findings.issue: useSensors is called on every render and creates new sensor instances. While @dnd-kit handles this internally, the PointerSensor and KeyboardSensor configs include object literals that create new references each render.
findings.recommendation: Extract sensor config to module-level constants: const POINTER_CONSTRAINT = { distance: 8 }; to avoid new object creation per render.

findings.issue: TodayHeader receives the full tasks array just to pass it to TaskJar. This means TodayHeader re-renders whenever any task changes, even though most of its props (totalCount, todayCompletedCount, ringTotal) are primitives that may not have changed.
findings.recommendation: Consider memoizing TodayHeader with React.memo, or pass only the data TaskJar needs (e.g., pre-filtered pending tasks) instead of the entire tasks array.
findings.code_snippet: <TodayHeader ... tasks={tasks} ... />

findings.issue: handleReschedule uses a sequential for...of loop with await — each overdue task is rescheduled one at a time. With 10 overdue tasks, this is 10 serial API calls.
findings.recommendation: Use Promise.all to parallelize: await Promise.all(overdueTasks.map(task => onUpdateTask(task.id, { dueDate: todayISO })));

findings.issue: Same sequential reschedule pattern as Upcoming.tsx — overdueTasks are rescheduled one at a time with await in a for loop.
findings.recommendation: Use Promise.all for parallel execution.

findings.issue: handleFinish iterates estimates Map and calls onUpdateTask sequentially in a for loop. With many task estimates, this creates serial API calls.
findings.recommendation: Batch the updates: collect all update promises and use Promise.all.

findings.issue: DraggableTaskCard is not wrapped in React.memo. Every quadrant re-render (e.g., from drag-over hover state) re-renders all task cards in that quadrant, even unchanged ones.
findings.recommendation: Wrap DraggableTaskCard in React.memo to skip re-renders when task, isSelected, and callbacks haven't changed.
findings.code_snippet: function DraggableTaskCard({ task, onSelect, isSelected }: { ... }) {

findings.issue: DraggableCard (Board view) is not wrapped in React.memo, same issue as Matrix.tsx. Drag operations cause all cards in a column to re-render.
findings.recommendation: Wrap DraggableCard in React.memo.
findings.code_snippet: function DraggableCard({ task, onToggle, onSelect, isSelected }: { ... }) {

findings.issue: new Date(task.dueDate!).toLocaleDateString(...) is called during render for each overdue task without memoization. toLocaleDateString is relatively expensive due to Intl formatting. With many overdue tasks, this adds up.
findings.recommendation: Pre-compute formatted dates in useMemo keyed on tasks, or use a simpler string-based date formatter.
findings.code_snippet:
new Date(task.dueDate!).toLocaleDateString(undefined, {
  month: "short",
  day: "numeric",
  year: "numeric",
  hour: "numeric",
  minute: "2-digit",
})

findings.issue: Completed view filters both 'completed' and 'cancelled' tasks (line 41) — this is likely a bug that also has perf implications. The Completed view shows cancelled tasks that should only appear in the Cancelled view, doubling rendered items.
findings.recommendation: Change the filter to only include t.status === 'completed' (remove the 'cancelled' check). This reduces rendered items and fixes a likely bug.
